rulings.Case Title: MARGARET SUE KAUFMAN VS. ROBIN GOLDMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Msk Hertiage'S Ntc Of Motion For Award Of Reasonable Atty'S Fees And Reasonable Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 3, 2010, line 4, PLAINTIFF MARGARET KAUFMAN MSK Hertiage's Motion for Award of Reasonable Attorney's Fees and Reasonable Costs. Motion for award of reasonable attorney fees and reasonable costs granted in part. Counsel's hourly rates are reasonable, however the requested fees are excessive. The case was relatively straightforward and involved only two motions, the demurrer and the cross-summary judgments. Counsel, who has over twenty years of litigation experience spent a disproportionate amount of time researching and reviewing case law, editing, and drafting. The court reduces plaintiffs' counsel's hours by 130 hours equal to a reduction of $45,5000, resulting in a reasonable award of attorney fees for reasonable and necessary work done in the amount of $47,315. The court reduces the costs to the reasonable amount of $3,027.80, disallowing $2,200 of the costs of the computer assisted research. Total fees and costs awarded is $50,342.80. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH ZADIK VS. DAVID CHOO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Amended Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 3, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANTS DAVID CHOO, CALIFORNIA MORTGAGE AND REALTY, INC., CMR MORTGAGE FUND II, LLC Hearing On Amended Petition To Compel Arbitration and to Stay Action. Motion granted. This motion is not a reconsideration motion, prior motion was denied without prejudice. Sufficient evidence presented that plaintiff was given the offering circular, which included the operating agreement containing the arbitration clause. Plaintiff executed the subscription agreements where he admitted to receiving and reviewing all documents. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KENDRA TOMCIK DR et al VS. SIDNEY FERNALD DR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 3, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT SIDNEY FERNALD DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. As to first cause of action for fraud, plaintiff must allege each element with particularity, and which alleged representations were false. As to the third cause of action, plaintiff must allege facts that show defendant did not have any intent to actually perform the advertised services, or that defendant promised to perform the services in a certain way or by a certain time. As to the fourth cause of action for recission, contradictory language indicates defendant had no intention of providing any of the services he promised, but plaintiff admits defendant did perform at least some of the services promised. Plaintiff has not sufficiently alleged failure of consideration. =(302/CWW)
